title: Reorder the port wire declarations to follow the same order of the port declarations
body:
_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_

In our applications, we often dump Verilog using `write_verilog`, make some small changes, and then use `read_verilog` to read it back to yosys. We found that the port ordering would change in this process (even when we make no changes). This creates a lot of problems for us. We conjecture that this is because `write_verilog` does not dump port wire declarations according to the order as the port declaraction part. So we make this change and find it solves our problem and we hope this may help others as well.

_Explain how this is achieved._

We add a vector to remember the order of signals when dumping the port declarations with `module` and then first dump these port wires following the order and then dump the other wires.

_If applicable, please suggest to reviewers how they can test the change._

You may dump some verilog and then read it back and see if the ports now follow the same order.
